author: Danny | World Market | ENO UK
msg:
After testing different device counts per box, here are the averages I’ve recorded (per device):
1 device → ~0.33 U
2 devices → ~0.26 U
3 devices → ~0.22 U
4 devices → ~0.21 U
5 devices → ~0.219 U (previous test average)
10 devices → 0.20 U average
14 devices → 0.2009 U average (2sims)


🔎 Key takeaway:
There is clear dilution as more devices share the same connection, but it’s not perfectly linear. Some mid-range setups (4–14 devices) appear to balance yield vs cost more efficiently.

author: Opher
msg: I disabled IPv6, Gave all 20 motherboards static IPs, lowered MTU size on Gateway, enabled UPnP, minimized firewall rules to the lowest security possible, and rebooted both gateway + box. My box was earning .02 average and now it’s at .22 average. I only did this for 2 boxes out of 4 in one of my locations(4boxes+4ISP) and those two boxes shot up the next day.
